services.title: Full Stack Website Development
services.description: End-to-end development of scalable web applications — from modern frontend interfaces to robust backend systems, built for performance, security, and long-term growth.
services.detailed_description: I architect and build complete web applications from the ground up — handling everything from database schema design and server-side logic to pixel-perfect frontend interfaces. Using Laravel 12 on the backend and React or Livewire on the frontend, I deliver systems that are fast, maintainable, and built to scale. Every project is engineered with clean code architecture, security best practices, and CI/CD-ready deployment in mind — so your product is production-ready from day one.
services_included: Custom web application development (Laravel + React / Livewire)
Single Page Applications (SPA) with Inertia.js
Server-Side Rendering (SSR) for SEO-critical applications
Multi-tenant SaaS architecture with isolated environments
Role-based access control and authentication (Laravel Sanctum / Passport)
Database design, Eloquent ORM, and query optimisation
Admin panel development (Laravel Nova / Filament)
Automated testing with PHPUnit and Pest
CI/CD pipeline setup and version control workflows
benefits: One engineer owning the full stack means fewer handoff gaps and faster delivery
Laravel + React is a battle-tested, enterprise-grade combination used in production by thousands of companies
Clean, documented code that your next developer can maintain without a full rewrite
Built for growth — architecture decisions made with scale in mind from day one
Agile delivery with incremental demos so you always know what's shipped
use_cases: A startup needing an MVP launched in 6–8 weeks with a clean API for a future mobile app
A business replacing a legacy PHP monolith with a modern Laravel + React system
An agency needing a white-label CMS platform for multiple clients
A SaaS company requiring a multi-tenant application with per-client billing and isolated data
problems_solved: Slow, unmaintainable codebases that developers are afraid to touch
Disconnected frontend and backend teams causing integration delays
Scaling bottlenecks when traffic or data volume grows unexpectedly
Security vulnerabilities from poorly structured authentication and data handling
Missed deadlines from unclear requirements and no iterative delivery

process.title: Discovery & scoping
process.description: Requirements gathering, technical audit (for existing systems), and a written specification with acceptance criteria.

process.title: Architecture & design
process.description: Database schema, API contracts, component structure, and tech stack decisions documented before a line of code is written.

process.title: Agile development
process.description: Two-week sprints with working demos at the end of each cycle. You see progress, not promises.

process.title: Testing & QA
process.description: Automated tests, manual QA, and cross-browser validation before any release.

process.title: Deployment & handover
process.description: Production deployment, environment configuration, documentation, and a handover session.

deliverables: Fully functional web application with documented codebase
API documentation (Postman collection or OpenAPI spec)
Database schema and migration files
Admin panel for content and user management
Deployment scripts and environment configuration
Technical handover documentation
outcomes: A production-ready application shipped on time and within scope
Codebase your team can maintain, extend, and hand off to new developers
Measurable performance benchmarks met (load time, uptime, response time)
Reduced operational overhead through automation and clean architecture
A clear technical foundation to build your next feature on

services.title: Mobile App Development
services.description: Cross-platform mobile applications with strong backend support — delivering smooth, responsive user experiences across iOS and Android from a single codebase.
services.detailed_description: I build cross-platform mobile applications using React Native and Flutter — the two leading frameworks backed by Meta and Google respectively — that run natively on both iOS and Android from a single codebase. This means faster delivery, lower maintenance cost, and a consistent experience for your users on every device. Every mobile app I build is connected to a robust backend API, whether Laravel-powered or third-party, and designed with offline capability, push notifications, and app store deployment in mind.
services_included: Cross-platform iOS and Android development (React Native / Flutter)
Mobile UI/UX implementation with platform-specific design conventions
Integration with Laravel or third-party REST APIs
Push notifications (Firebase Cloud Messaging)
Offline mode and local data sync
In-app payments and subscription management
Social and OAuth authentication
Google Maps and location-based features
App Store and Google Play submission and deployment
benefits: One codebase for iOS and Android — 30–50% faster to build and cheaper to maintain than two native apps
Near-native performance with React Native's New Architecture and Flutter's Impeller engine
Seamlessly connected to your existing web backend — no duplicate business logic
Built to App Store and Play Store guidelines from the start — no last-minute rejections
Consistent user experience across all devices and screen sizes
use_cases: A startup wanting a mobile app that shares a Laravel backend with their existing web platform
An e-commerce business needing a mobile storefront with push notifications and in-app checkout
A service provider building an on-demand booking app for customers and a separate app for field staff
An internal enterprise tool replacing paper-based or desktop-only workflows with a mobile-first interface
problems_solved: High cost and complexity of maintaining separate iOS and Android native codebases
Mobile app that doesn't match the quality and performance of the web product
App store rejections due to guideline violations caught late in development
Poor offline experience causing user frustration in low-connectivity environments
Disconnected mobile and web data — users seeing different information on different devices

process.title: Scope & platform decision
process.description: Define features, target platforms, and choose React Native or Flutter based on project requirements and team context.

process.title: UI design implementation
process.description: Build screens using platform-appropriate components — iOS Human Interface Guidelines and Android Material Design respected.

process.title: Backend integration
process.description: Connect to APIs, configure authentication, set up push notifications and data sync.

process.title: Device testing
process.description: Test on real devices across multiple OS versions and screen sizes — not just simulators.

process.title: Store submission
process.description: Prepare app store assets, privacy policies, and submit to App Store and Google Play on your behalf.

deliverables: Fully functional iOS and Android application
Source code repository with clear documentation
App Store and Google Play listings with screenshots and descriptions
Push notification setup and configuration
API integration documentation
Post-launch bug fix period
outcomes: Live app available to users on both major platforms simultaneously
Single codebase that reduces future update and maintenance cost by up to 50%
Higher user engagement through push notifications and offline capability
A mobile presence that matches the quality standard of your web product
Clear upgrade path for adding new features incrementally

services.title: E-commerce Development
services.description: Custom e-commerce solutions built for conversions and performance — including WooCommerce and Shopify stores with product management, secure payment integration, and scalable storefront architecture.
services.detailed_description: I build e-commerce platforms that are engineered to convert visitors into customers and scale as your catalogue and traffic grow. Whether you need a WooCommerce store with complex product logic, a Shopify storefront with custom theme development, or a fully bespoke Laravel-powered marketplace, I handle the complete build — from storefront design to payment gateway integration, inventory management, and post-launch support. Every store is optimised for speed, mobile experience, and search engine visibility from the start.
services_included: WooCommerce store setup, custom themes, and plugin development
Shopify theme development and app integration
Custom Laravel e-commerce platforms and marketplaces
Payment gateway integration (Razorpay, Stripe, PayPal, PayU)
Product catalogue management and bulk import tools
Multi-currency and multi-language storefronts
Abandoned cart recovery and email automation
Inventory and order management systems
Customer account portals and loyalty programmes
benefits: Conversion-focused architecture — fast load times, streamlined checkout, and mobile-first design
Secure, PCI-compliant payment handling with multiple gateway options
Flexible product management — simple products, variants, bundles, subscriptions, digital downloads
Built-in SEO foundations — structured data, sitemap, canonical URLs, and Core Web Vitals optimised
Scales cleanly from 10 products to 10,000 without a platform rebuild
use_cases: A retail brand launching an online store with complex product variants and booking options
A pooja samagri or religious products business needing an online storefront with COD and UPI support
A B2B wholesaler needing a private, login-gated catalogue with customer-specific pricing
A creator or service provider selling digital products, courses, or bookings online
problems_solved: Slow storefront load times losing customers before the first product page loads
Checkout friction causing cart abandonment — too many steps, limited payment options
Inventory mismatches between the website and actual stock
Poor mobile experience on a store where 70%+ of traffic comes from phones
No visibility into sales data, customer behaviour, or inventory health

process.title: Platform selection
process.description: Recommend WooCommerce, Shopify, or custom Laravel based on your catalogue size, budget, and required features.

process.title: Store setup & design
process.description: Configure the platform, implement or customise the theme, and build the product catalogue structure.

process.title: Integrations
process.description: Connect payment gateways, shipping providers, inventory systems, and email marketing tools.

process.title: Testing & optimisation
process.description: Full checkout flow testing, mobile testing, page speed optimisation, and security hardening.

process.title: Launch & support
process.description: Go-live support, staff training on store management, and a post-launch support window.

deliverables: Fully functional e-commerce store on your chosen platform
Payment gateway integration and test transaction confirmation
Product catalogue with categories, variants, and pricing configured
Admin training documentation for managing products and orders
Speed and SEO optimisation report
Post-launch support period
outcomes: A store that loads in under 3 seconds and converts mobile visitors at a competitive rate
Secure, reliable checkout that customers trust — reducing cart abandonment
Full control over your product catalogue, inventory, and orders without developer dependency
SEO-ready foundation that can rank for product and category keywords
Scalable infrastructure ready for seasonal traffic spikes and catalogue growth

services.title: SaaS & MVP Development
services.description: Rapid development of MVPs and SaaS platforms — turning ideas into production-ready products with scalable architecture and clean, maintainable code.
services.detailed_description: I specialise in turning well-defined product ideas into working software quickly — without cutting corners on architecture. Using Laravel for the backend, React or Livewire for the frontend, and AWS for infrastructure, I build MVPs that are genuinely production-ready: multi-tenant where needed, API-first, and designed to support your next ten features without a rewrite. Whether you are a founder validating an idea or a business spinning up an internal SaaS tool, I deliver working software, not prototypes.
services_included: MVP scoping and feature prioritisation
Multi-tenant SaaS architecture (database-per-tenant or shared schema)
Subscription billing integration (Stripe / Razorpay with Laravel Cashier)
User onboarding flows and account management
Role and permission systems
Analytics and usage dashboards
API-first architecture for future mobile or integration use
Automated email workflows and notification systems
Staging and production environment setup on AWS
benefits: Go from idea to working product in 6–12 weeks — fast enough to validate before competitors
API-first architecture means your MVP can power a mobile app or third-party integration on day one
Subscription billing and user management built-in — revenue-ready from launch
Clean codebase that a future team can extend without burning it down
Agile process means you can pivot based on early user feedback without wasted build
use_cases: A founder needing a working SaaS demo to show investors within 8 weeks
A business automating an internal workflow that currently runs on spreadsheets
A club or association needing a members portal with activity tracking and analytics
A startup building a sales-enablement or eDetailing platform for field teams
problems_solved: Ideas stuck in planning because building feels too big and too expensive
Prototypes that work in demos but break under real usage or real data
SaaS platforms that become unsaleable because of poor multi-tenancy or billing architecture
MVPs that can't be extended — every new feature requires a rewrite of existing code
Long development cycles with no visible progress until the end

process.title: Product scoping
process.description: Define the core problem, the must-have feature set for launch, and what to defer to v2. Written spec with acceptance criteria.

process.title: Architecture planning
process.description: Database schema, multi-tenancy model, API structure, and billing flow designed before coding starts.

process.title: Sprint development
process.description: Two-week sprints with a working, reviewable build at the end of each cycle.

process.title: Beta & feedback
process.description: Deploy to staging, onboard beta users, and iterate based on real usage data.

process.title: Production launch
process.description: AWS deployment, monitoring setup, go-live support, and handover documentation.

deliverables: Production-ready SaaS application with documented codebase
Multi-tenant or single-tenant architecture as specified
Stripe or Razorpay subscription billing integration
Admin and user dashboards
API documentation
AWS deployment and environment configuration
Post-launch support period
outcomes: A working product in the hands of real users — not a Figma file or a prototype
Revenue-ready platform with subscription billing from launch day
Architecture that supports 10x user growth without structural rework
Codebase your next hire can onboard to in days, not weeks
Validated learning about your market before investing in a full product team

services.title: Cloud & DevOps (AWS)
services.description: Deployment, server configuration, and cloud infrastructure on AWS — ensuring your applications are secure, scalable, and highly available.
services.detailed_description: I configure and manage production cloud infrastructure on AWS — setting up the environments your application needs to be secure, performant, and resilient. From initial server setup and SSL configuration to S3 asset pipelines and Lambda-powered serverless functions, I handle the infrastructure layer so your development team can focus on shipping features. I also implement CI/CD pipelines, automated backups, and monitoring — giving you confidence that your production environment is production-grade.
services_included: AWS EC2 server setup and configuration (Ubuntu / Amazon Linux)
Nginx and Apache web server configuration
SSL certificate setup and auto-renewal (Let's Encrypt / ACM)
S3 bucket setup for file storage and static asset delivery
AWS Lambda serverless function development and deployment
CloudFront CDN configuration for global asset delivery
RDS database setup and configuration (MySQL / PostgreSQL)
CI/CD pipeline setup (GitHub Actions / Bitbucket Pipelines)
Server monitoring, alerting, and automated backups
benefits: AWS infrastructure used by millions of businesses globally — reliable, secure, and well-documented
Pay-as-you-scale pricing — no over-provisioning for traffic you don't have yet
Automated deployments reduce human error and make releasing software a non-event
S3 and CloudFront deliver assets faster to users globally without touching your server
Monitoring and alerting means you know about problems before your users do
use_cases: A Laravel application that needs a production-grade server setup with zero-downtime deployments
A media platform needing S3 storage and CloudFront CDN for fast global video or image delivery
A startup that needs serverless Lambda functions for background processing tasks
An existing application on shared hosting that needs to migrate to a dedicated AWS environment
problems_solved: Applications going down under traffic spikes because of under-configured servers
Security vulnerabilities from misconfigured servers, open ports, and missing SSL
Slow asset delivery because files are being served directly from the application server
Manual, error-prone deployments that cause downtime every time code is released
No visibility into server health, error rates, or response times until users complain

process.title: Infrastructure audit
process.description: Review current setup (or start from scratch), define requirements for compute, storage, database, and networking.

process.title: Environment setup
process.description: Provision EC2 instances, configure web server, set up RDS, S3, and security groups.

process.title: Application deployment
process.description: Deploy the application, configure environment variables, set up domain and SSL.

process.title: CI/CD pipeline
process.description: Automate deployments via GitHub Actions or Bitbucket Pipelines — push to main, deploy to production.

process.title: Monitoring & handover
process.description: Set up uptime monitoring, error alerting, automated backups, and hand over access and documentation.

deliverables: Configured AWS environment (EC2, RDS, S3, CloudFront as applicable)
Web server configuration files (Nginx/Apache)
CI/CD pipeline configuration
SSL setup with auto-renewal
Monitoring and alerting setup
Infrastructure documentation and access credentials handover
outcomes: Production environment that handles traffic spikes without downtime
Zero-downtime deployments — releasing code is no longer a high-stress event
Secure infrastructure with properly configured firewalls, SSL, and access controls
Faster asset delivery to users globally through CDN
Full visibility into server health and application performance

services.title: SEO & Performance Optimisation
services.description: On-page SEO implementation, Core Web Vitals tuning, database query optimisation, and caching strategy — so your product loads faster, ranks higher, and scales cleanly.
services.detailed_description: I approach SEO and performance as an engineering problem, not a checklist. Fast load times, clean HTML structure, proper meta and structured data implementation, and a well-indexed site architecture are the foundations of sustainable organic search performance. I diagnose and fix Core Web Vitals failures, implement server-side caching, optimise slow database queries, and ensure your application performs under load — because a site that loads in 1 second converts measurably better than one that loads in 3.
services_included: Core Web Vitals audit and remediation (LCP, INP, CLS)
On-page SEO implementation — meta tags, canonical URLs, Open Graph, structured data (JSON-LD)
XML sitemap generation and Google Search Console setup
Page speed optimisation — image compression, lazy loading, code minification, asset bundling
Server-side caching implementation (Laravel Cache, Redis, OPcache)
Database query optimisation and indexing strategy
Technical SEO audit — crawl errors, redirect chains, duplicate content, broken links
WordPress Yoast/RankMath configuration and content SEO guidelines
Performance monitoring setup (Google PageSpeed Insights, Lighthouse CI)
benefits: Faster load times directly correlate with higher conversion rates — every second counts
Google ranks fast, well-structured sites higher — performance is an SEO advantage
Structured data and proper meta implementation improve click-through rates from search results
Database and caching optimisation reduce server load and hosting costs under scale
One-time technical improvements compound over time — unlike paid ads
use_cases: A WooCommerce store with a poor Google PageSpeed score losing rankings to faster competitors
A WordPress blog with thousands of posts needing technical SEO cleanup and structured data
A Laravel application with slow database queries causing timeouts under moderate traffic
A new website needing a complete SEO foundation set up before launch
problems_solved: Poor Core Web Vitals scores causing ranking penalties in Google Search
Slow database queries timing out under normal traffic loads
Unindexed pages, crawl errors, and duplicate content diluting search authority
Images and assets served uncompressed, destroying load time on mobile
No caching strategy means every page request hits the database and server fresh

process.title: Audit
process.description: Full technical SEO and performance audit using Lighthouse, Google Search Console, PageSpeed Insights, and database profiling tools.

process.title: Prioritisation
process.description: Rank issues by impact — fix the problems that move metrics the most, first.

process.title: Technical fixes
process.description: Implement caching, optimise queries, fix crawl errors, compress assets, and resolve Core Web Vitals issues.

process.title: On-page SEO
process.description: Implement structured data, meta tags, canonical URLs, sitemap, and Search Console configuration.

process.title: Reporting
process.description: Deliver before/after performance benchmarks and a monitoring setup so you can track improvements over time.

deliverables: Technical SEO and performance audit report with prioritised fix list
Core Web Vitals improvement (LCP, INP, CLS benchmarks documented)
Caching and query optimisation implementation
Structured data and on-page SEO implementation
Google Search Console and sitemap setup
Before/after PageSpeed Insights and Lighthouse scores
outcomes: Measurable improvement in Core Web Vitals — LCP under 2.5s, CLS under 0.1, INP under 200ms
Higher organic search rankings driven by improved technical SEO signals
Reduced server load and infrastructure cost through caching
Faster pages with higher user engagement, lower bounce rates, and better conversions
A clean technical foundation that future content and SEO efforts can build on

services.title: API Development & Integration
services.description: Design and integration of RESTful APIs — enabling seamless communication between systems, third-party services, and web or mobile applications.
services.detailed_description: I design, build, and integrate APIs that connect your systems cleanly and reliably. Whether you need a RESTful API backend for your mobile app, a set of webhooks to sync data between platforms, or integration with third-party services like payment gateways, CRMs, or marketing tools, I architect the data contracts and write the integration code that makes it work — and keeps working when the external service changes. Every API I build is versioned, documented, and secured.
services_included: RESTful API design and development (Laravel API / Lumen)
API versioning and backward-compatible upgrade strategy
Authentication and authorisation (Sanctum, Passport, JWT, OAuth2)
Webhook development and inbound webhook handling
Third-party API integration (payment gateways, CRMs, marketing tools)
API rate limiting, throttling, and abuse prevention
API documentation (Postman collections, OpenAPI / Swagger spec)
API testing and automated contract testing
GraphQL API development for flexible data querying
benefits: Well-designed APIs let your web app, mobile app, and third-party tools all share one source of truth
Versioned APIs mean you can update your backend without breaking existing integrations
Documented APIs with Postman collections reduce onboarding time for every future developer
Proper authentication means your data is only accessible to the systems that should have it
Webhook-driven integrations reduce polling overhead and keep data in sync in near real-time
use_cases: A mobile app that needs a secure, well-structured Laravel API to power its features
An e-commerce platform integrating with Razorpay, Shiprocket, and a CRM simultaneously
A SaaS platform that needs to expose a public API so enterprise clients can integrate
A business replacing manual data entry by syncing two internal systems via webhooks
problems_solved: Brittle integrations that break every time a third-party API changes its response format
Unsecured API endpoints exposing sensitive data without proper authentication
No API documentation, making it impossible to onboard new developers or build a mobile app
Duplicate data across systems because there is no reliable sync mechanism
Slow API responses caused by N+1 query problems and missing eager loading

process.title: API design
process.description: Define resource structure, endpoints, request/response contracts, and authentication strategy before writing code.

process.title: Development
process.description: Build API endpoints with proper error handling, validation, rate limiting, and logging.

process.title: Third-party integration
process.description: Connect to external services, handle webhooks, and build retry logic for reliability.

process.title: Testing
process.description: Automated API tests, edge case validation, and load testing for high-traffic endpoints.

process.title: Documentation & handover
process.description: Postman collection or OpenAPI spec delivered alongside the codebase.

deliverables: Fully functional REST API with versioning
Authentication and authorisation implementation
Postman collection or OpenAPI documentation
Third-party integration code with error handling and logging
Automated API test suite
Rate limiting and security configuration
outcomes: A reliable API that your mobile app, web frontend, and partners can all depend on
Reduced manual data entry through automated system-to-system sync
Secure data access with proper authentication — no more exposed endpoints
Documentation that cuts new developer onboarding time significantly
A foundation for building a partner ecosystem or public API offering

services.title: WordPress & WooCommerce
services.description: Custom themes, bespoke plugins, and Bedrock-based WordPress setups tailored to your workflow — including WooCommerce stores with complex booking logic, dynamic data visualisations, and editorial pipelines.
services.detailed_description: I build WordPress solutions that go well beyond off-the-shelf themes and plugins. Using Bedrock for a modern, Composer-driven project structure and ACF for flexible content architecture, I deliver WordPress sites that are maintainable, secure, and built to your exact editorial and functional requirements. For WooCommerce, I handle everything from complex product configurations and custom booking rules to payment gateway integration and performance optimisation for large catalogues.
services_included: Custom WordPress theme development (from scratch or child theme)
Bedrock-based modern WordPress project setup (Composer, environment management)
Advanced Custom Fields (ACF) and Gutenberg block development
Custom WordPress plugin development
WooCommerce store setup with custom product types and checkout flows
Complex booking and reservation system implementation
WordPress REST API integration with external systems
Dynamic charts and data visualisation in WordPress
WordPress multisite setup and management
benefits: Bedrock project structure means consistent deployments across dev, staging, and production environments
Custom themes built to your brand — not adapted from a generic template someone else is using
Editorial teams get an intuitive CMS experience without needing developer involvement for content updates
Custom plugins built specifically for your workflow — no bloat from generic solutions
WooCommerce on WordPress gives you one platform for content and commerce
use_cases: A digital marketing agency needing a custom Bedrock WordPress showcase site that reflects their technical credibility
A government department publishing energy or environmental data with dynamic charts and nested JSON feeds
A religious or cultural services business needing a WooCommerce site with pandit booking and product sales
A business needing a bespoke WordPress plugin to automate an editorial or approval workflow
problems_solved: WordPress sites that are slow, bloated, and reliant on 30+ plugins for basic functionality
Editorial workflows that require a developer every time content needs to change
WooCommerce stores with product or checkout logic that standard plugins cannot handle
Inconsistent deployments caused by manual file uploads instead of version-controlled code
Security vulnerabilities from outdated themes, plugins, and WordPress core versions

process.title: Requirements & content architecture
process.description: Map content types, editorial workflow, and WooCommerce product/checkout requirements.

process.title: Theme & plugin development
process.description: Build custom theme and any required plugins — all version-controlled in a Bedrock project structure.

process.title: Content entry & configuration
process.description: Set up ACF fields, configure WooCommerce, enter initial content, and configure payment gateways.

process.title: Testing
process.description: Cross-browser testing, mobile testing, WooCommerce checkout testing, and performance audit.

process.title: Launch & training
process.description: Deploy to production, train the editorial team on the CMS, and provide documentation.

deliverables: Custom WordPress theme (version-controlled, documented)
Custom plugins as specified
Bedrock project configuration with environment files
WooCommerce setup with payment gateway integration
Editorial team training documentation
Performance and security hardening report
outcomes: A WordPress site your editorial team can manage independently — no developer dependency for content
Consistent, reproducible deployments across all environments
WooCommerce store handling your specific product and checkout logic reliably
Performance optimised site with fast load times and clean Core Web Vitals scores
A maintainable codebase that any WordPress developer can extend

services.title: Shopify Development
services.description: Custom Shopify storefronts and theme development — tailored to your brand, optimised for conversions, and built to scale as your business grows.
services.detailed_description: I build Shopify stores that perform and convert — custom-themed to your brand, integrated with the apps and systems your business runs on, and optimised for the speed and experience that turns browsers into buyers. Whether you are launching a new store, migrating from WooCommerce or another platform, or needing a full custom Shopify theme built with Online Store 2.0 and Liquid, I handle the complete build and deliver a store your team can manage confidently.
services_included: Custom Shopify theme development (Liquid, Online Store 2.0, Sections Everywhere)
Shopify theme customisation and existing theme modification
WooCommerce to Shopify migration (products, orders, customers)
Shopify app integration and configuration
Custom Shopify app development (Shopify CLI / Node.js)
Shopify Markets setup for multi-currency and multi-language
Checkout customisation (Shopify Plus)
Product page and collection page optimisation for conversions
Shopify analytics and Google Shopping feed setup
benefits: Shopify handles hosting, SSL, and platform maintenance — you focus on selling, not servers
Custom Online Store 2.0 themes give merchants full editorial control without touching code
Built-in payment processing, shipping integrations, and tax calculations reduce setup complexity
Shopify's CDN delivers fast load times globally out of the box
The Shopify app ecosystem covers almost every business need without custom development
use_cases: A fashion or lifestyle brand launching a premium online store that needs to reflect their brand identity — not a default template
A WooCommerce merchant overwhelmed by plugin maintenance wanting to migrate to a simpler, managed platform
A business selling in multiple countries needing Shopify Markets for localised pricing and currency
An existing Shopify merchant whose store has performance or conversion issues that need a developer to fix

Poor mobile checkout experience causing high abandonment rates on phones
Slow product pages caused by too many unoptimised apps loading scripts on every page
WooCommerce migration anxiety — product data, orders, and customer records all need to move cleanly

process.title: Store strategy
process.description: Review brand guidelines, product catalogue, target market, and identify required apps and integrations.

process.title: Theme development
process.description: Build the custom Liquid theme with Online Store 2.0 sections — editable by your team without code.

process.title: Migration & setup
process.description: Migrate products, collections, and customer data. Configure apps, payment methods, and shipping rules.

process.title: Conversion optimisation

process.title: Launch & training

deliverables: Custom Shopify theme (Liquid, Online Store 2.0, fully editable)
Product catalogue and collection setup
Payment, shipping, and tax configuration
App integrations as specified
Merchant training documentation
Post-launch support period
outcomes: A high-converting Shopify store that reflects your brand — not a template
Merchant team fully capable of managing products, collections, and content independently
Fast, mobile-optimised storefront with Shopify's global CDN
Smooth migration from the previous platform with no data loss
A platform your business can grow on without needing constant developer support

services.title: Frontend Engineering
services.description: Responsive, accessible interfaces built with React, Tailwind, and Bootstrap — performant from the first load and wired cleanly to any backend or third-party API.
services.detailed_description: I build frontend interfaces that are fast, accessible, and a pleasure to use — and that stay maintainable as your product grows. Using React with component-driven architecture, Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling, and modern tooling like Vite and TypeScript, I deliver frontends that perform on every device, integrate cleanly with any backend API, and follow accessibility standards. Whether you need a standalone React SPA, a Livewire-powered Laravel frontend, or a React component library for your design system, I build it right.
services_included: React SPA development (Vite, React Router, React Query)
Laravel + Inertia.js SPA (no separate API required)
Laravel Livewire for reactive server-driven UI
Tailwind CSS implementation and design system setup
Responsive design across mobile, tablet, and desktop
Component library development and Storybook documentation
Dashboard and data visualisation UI (charts, tables, filters)
Form validation, error handling, and UX micro-interactions
Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) implementation and audit
benefits: Component-driven architecture means every UI element is reusable, testable, and easy to update
Tailwind CSS eliminates unused CSS and enforces consistent spacing, colour, and typography
React Query and proper state management eliminate the most common frontend performance problems
Accessibility compliance opens your product to a wider audience and reduces legal risk
Inertia.js and Livewire options mean you get SPA-like experience without the complexity of a separate API
use_cases: A SaaS product needing a React dashboard with complex data tables, filters, and real-time updates
A Laravel application whose Blade templates need replacing with a modern, reactive Livewire or Inertia frontend
A design team that has Figma mockups and needs an engineer to translate them into pixel-perfect React components
An enterprise tool with a functional but dated UI that needs a frontend redesign without touching the backend
problems_solved: Slow, janky frontend UX caused by poor state management and unnecessary re-renders
Inconsistent UI — every page looks slightly different because there is no component system
Inaccessible interfaces that fail on keyboard navigation, screen readers, or colour contrast
Tightly coupled frontend code that makes every change risky and slow
Mobile experience that is an afterthought — designed desktop-first and broken on phones

process.title: Design review
process.description: Review Figma designs or existing UI, identify component boundaries, and plan the component hierarchy.

process.title: Project setup
process.description: Configure Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind, ESLint, and folder structure for the project scale.

process.title: Component development
process.description: Build components from atoms up — design tokens, base components, composite components, page layouts.

process.title: API integration
process.description: Connect to backend APIs with React Query or Axios, implement loading states, error boundaries, and optimistic updates.

process.title: QA & performance
process.description: Cross-browser testing, Lighthouse performance audit, accessibility audit, and code review.

deliverables: React or Livewire frontend codebase (TypeScript, documented components)
Tailwind CSS configuration and design token setup
Component library with usage documentation
API integration layer with error handling
Lighthouse performance and accessibility report
Responsive implementation tested across devices
outcomes: A fast, consistent UI that your users actually enjoy using
Component library that speeds up every future feature addition
Accessible interface that works for all users and meets compliance requirements
Frontend codebase any React developer can onboard to and extend
Performance benchmarks that support strong Core Web Vitals scores

services.title: WebSocket & Real-Time Features
services.description: Live notifications, real-time data feeds, and event-driven systems — built with WebSocket to keep your users connected and your data always current.
services.detailed_description: I implement real-time features that transform static applications into live, responsive experiences. Using Laravel Broadcasting with Pusher or Laravel Reverb (the new first-party WebSocket server), I build event-driven systems that push data to connected clients the moment it changes — without polling, without page refreshes, and without complexity for the end user. From live notifications and chat to real-time dashboards and collaborative tools, I design and build the full stack of the real-time feature.
services_included: Laravel Broadcasting with Pusher or Laravel Reverb setup
Real-time notification systems (in-app and push)
Live data dashboards with auto-updating charts and tables
Chat and messaging features
Presence channels (who is currently online / viewing)
Real-time form collaboration
WebSocket connection management, reconnection, and fallback handling
Event-driven architecture with Laravel queues and jobs
Laravel Echo (JavaScript client) integration
benefits: Real-time updates eliminate the need for users to refresh — better UX and higher engagement
WebSocket is dramatically more efficient than polling — fewer server requests, lower infrastructure cost
Laravel Reverb is a first-party, self-hosted WebSocket solution — no third-party dependency or per-message pricing
Event-driven architecture decouples your application — adding a new real-time feature doesn't require rewriting existing code
Presence channels let you build collaborative features — showing who is online or viewing the same record
use_cases: A SaaS platform where multiple users edit shared data and need to see each other's changes in real time
A logistics dashboard showing live vehicle positions, delivery statuses, and alerts without page refresh
A helpdesk or support tool where agents and customers exchange messages in a real-time chat interface
An e-commerce site showing live stock levels on product pages to create urgency
problems_solved: Users working on stale data because the page was loaded 10 minutes ago and nothing has updated
High server load from constant polling — hundreds of clients each requesting fresh data every few seconds
Real-time features that work in demos but fail under concurrent users in production
Notification systems that deliver alerts minutes after the triggering event
Collaborative tools where users overwrite each other's work because there is no live presence awareness

process.title: Event architecture
process.description: Define which events need to broadcast, to which channels, and to which users — before writing any WebSocket code.

process.title: Server setup
process.description: Configure Laravel Reverb or Pusher, set up broadcasting channels with proper authentication.

process.title: Event broadcasting
process.description: Implement Laravel events, listeners, and broadcast conditions on the backend.

process.title: Frontend integration
process.description: Connect with Laravel Echo on the frontend, update UI reactively when events arrive.

process.title: Load testing
process.description: Test with concurrent connections to validate performance under realistic usage.

deliverables: WebSocket server setup (Reverb or Pusher)
Laravel Broadcasting event and channel implementation
Laravel Echo frontend integration
Real-time UI components (notifications, live data tables, chat)
Connection resilience and reconnection handling
Load test results documentation
outcomes: Users see data updates the moment they happen — no refresh required
Reduced server load compared to polling-based approaches
Higher user engagement through live notifications and collaborative features
Reliable real-time features that hold up under concurrent users
Event-driven foundation that makes adding future real-time features fast and clean

services.title: AI-Enhanced Development
services.description: From embedding LLM-powered features — chat, summarisation, classification — into your product, to automating workflows and business logic using AI tools. Faster delivery, smarter systems, less manual overhead.
services.detailed_description: I integrate AI capabilities into web and mobile products at two levels. The first is product-level: embedding LLM-powered features — intelligent chat, document summarisation, content classification, automated data extraction — directly into your application via APIs like OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, or Gemini. The second is workflow-level: using AI tools to accelerate development, automate business logic, and reduce the manual overhead that slows teams down. Both compound over time — better products built faster.
services_included: LLM API integration (OpenAI GPT-4, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini)
AI-powered chat and conversational interface development
Document and content summarisation features
Text classification, tagging, and content moderation
AI-assisted data extraction from unstructured content
Prompt engineering and system prompt design
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) with vector databases
AI workflow automation for internal business processes
AI-augmented code generation and development tooling setup
benefits: AI features differentiate your product in a market where basic functionality is commoditised
LLM APIs abstract the complexity of machine learning — you get the capability without building models
AI-assisted development accelerates delivery — features that would take weeks are scoped and built faster
Automated classification and extraction reduces manual data entry and human error
RAG-based features let your product answer questions using your own proprietary knowledge base
use_cases: A SaaS CRM that needs an AI assistant to summarise customer history and suggest next actions
A legal tech platform that needs to extract key clauses from uploaded contracts automatically
A support helpdesk adding an AI first-response layer that resolves common tickets without human involvement
An e-commerce site adding AI-powered semantic search so customers find products by describing what they need
problems_solved: Repetitive manual tasks — classification, tagging, data entry — consuming team time that could be automated
Search that only works if the user knows the exact product name or SKU
Support volume that scales with user growth and cannot be handled by a fixed-size team
Slow development cycles from writing boilerplate code that AI tooling can generate in seconds
Proprietary knowledge locked in documents and databases that users cannot query conversationally

process.title: AI feature scoping
process.description: Define the specific problem AI should solve, choose the right model and approach (chat, classification, RAG, etc.).

process.title: Prompt engineering
process.description: Design and test system prompts and user prompt structures to get reliable, accurate model outputs.

process.title: API integration
process.description: Integrate the chosen LLM API into your application with proper authentication, error handling, and cost controls.

process.title: Testing & evaluation
process.description: Test outputs across edge cases, measure accuracy, and tune prompts for production reliability.

process.title: Deployment & monitoring
process.description: Deploy with usage monitoring and cost alerts — AI API costs scale with usage and need visibility.

deliverables: LLM API integration (production-ready with error handling and retry logic)
Prompt library and system prompt documentation
AI feature UI (chat interface, classification output display, etc.)
Cost monitoring and usage alerts setup
Evaluation report on accuracy and output quality
Documentation for extending and maintaining the AI integration
outcomes: AI-powered features live in your product that users can interact with immediately
Measurable reduction in manual tasks through automation
Faster development velocity through AI-assisted tooling
A competitive product differentiator that is hard for non-technical competitors to replicate
Clear understanding of AI API costs with monitoring to prevent surprise billing

services.title: Custom CMS Development
services.description: Purpose-built content management systems in Laravel — designed around your editorial workflow, not the other way around. Clean interfaces, structured data, and APIs your frontend team will enjoy working with.
services.detailed_description: Off-the-shelf CMS platforms force your editorial workflow to fit their data model. I build custom CMS systems in Laravel that are designed around exactly how your team works — the content types you have, the approval workflows you need, the APIs your frontend consumes, and the permissions model that matches your organisation. Whether you need a headless CMS powering a React frontend, a full-stack content platform with its own editorial UI, or a Laravel CMS with Filament or Nova admin panel, I build it to fit.
services_included: Custom Laravel CMS with tailored content types and schemas
Filament or Laravel Nova admin panel implementation
Headless CMS with RESTful or GraphQL API for frontend consumption
Multi-role editorial workflow (draft, review, publish, schedule)
Media library and asset management
Content versioning and revision history
Multi-language and multi-region content management
Dynamic form builder and custom field types
Analytics integration and content performance dashboards
benefits: Content types, fields, and workflows built exactly for your data — no workarounds or plugin hacks
Headless architecture means your content powers any frontend — web, mobile, kiosk, or all three
Fine-grained role and permission control — editors, reviewers, admins, and read-only users all handled correctly
Scheduled publishing, content versioning, and revision history give editors confidence and control
API-first design means any future frontend or integration can consume your content without a CMS rebuild
use_cases: A government department publishing structured data (energy, weather, policy) that WordPress cannot model cleanly
A media company needing a custom editorial workflow with multi-stage approval before articles go live
A SaaS product that needs an embedded CMS for its own users to manage their content within the platform
A club or association managing member activity, event scheduling, and communications through one custom-built admin interface
problems_solved: WordPress or other CMS platforms that cannot model your content types without heavy plugin customisation
Editorial teams blocked by developers for every content change because the CMS is not built for their workflow
Content that lives in one place but needs to power multiple channels — and currently has to be duplicated manually
No approval workflow — content goes live immediately without review, causing quality and compliance issues
Slow CMS admin interfaces caused by unoptimised queries on large content databases

process.title: Content modelling
process.description: Map all content types, fields, relationships, and workflow states before any development begins.

process.title: Schema & API design
process.description: Design the database schema and API contracts the frontend will consume.

process.title: Admin panel development
process.description: Build the editorial interface with Filament or Nova — intuitive, fast, and role-aware.

process.title: API development
process.description: Build the content delivery API with filtering, pagination, search, and caching.

process.title: Training & launch
process.description: Train the editorial team, document the content model, and support the go-live.

deliverables: Custom Laravel CMS with documented content types and schemas
Filament or Nova admin panel configured for your editorial team
Content delivery API (REST or GraphQL) with documentation
Role and permission configuration
Editorial team training documentation
Content migration from previous system (if applicable)
outcomes: Editorial team publishing and managing content independently — no developer bottleneck
Structured content that can power any current or future frontend channel
Approval workflows that prevent unreviewed content from going live
A CMS that is fast, intuitive, and built around how your team actually works
API foundation that makes future integrations and channel additions straightforward

services.title: Database Design & Optimisation
services.description: Scalable relational schema design, query optimisation, and indexing strategy — built to handle growth without performance degradation.
services.detailed_description: A poorly designed database is the most common reason web applications become slow and expensive to change. I design relational database schemas that are normalised correctly, indexed appropriately, and structured so that your most frequent queries are fast from day one. For existing applications, I audit slow queries, identify missing indexes, fix N+1 problems, and implement caching layers — delivering measurable performance improvements without requiring a full application rewrite.
services_included: Relational database schema design (MySQL / PostgreSQL)
Database normalisation and data integrity enforcement
Indexing strategy — composite indexes, covering indexes, partial indexes
Query optimisation and Eloquent ORM performance tuning
N+1 query detection and eager loading implementation
Database caching with Redis (query cache, model cache)
Database migration planning and zero-downtime migration execution
Slow query log analysis and EXPLAIN plan interpretation
PostgreSQL advanced features — JSONB, full-text search, partitioning
industries: Any application with large or growing datasets
benefits: A well-indexed schema means queries stay fast as your data grows from thousands to millions of rows
Fixing N+1 queries can reduce page load times by 50–90% without changing any business logic
Redis caching eliminates repeat database reads for frequently accessed, rarely changing data
Correct normalisation prevents data inconsistencies and reduces the risk of bugs from stale data
Zero-downtime migration strategies let you change the database without taking your application offline
use_cases: A SaaS application whose database queries are getting slower as the customer count grows
An analytics platform running complex aggregation queries that time out under normal usage
A new application that needs a database schema designed correctly before the first line of business logic is written
An existing application migrating from MySQL to PostgreSQL to take advantage of JSONB and full-text search
problems_solved: Application pages timing out because a single poorly optimised query is scanning the entire table
Data inconsistencies caused by denormalised schemas that store the same information in multiple places
Database becoming the bottleneck — CPU and memory spiking under moderate traffic
Developers afraid to change the schema because there is no migration strategy and the data cannot be taken offline
Reports and dashboards that take minutes to load because aggregation queries have no caching or optimisation

process.title: Audit
process.description: Analyse slow query logs, run EXPLAIN plans on the worst-performing queries, and map the current schema.

process.title: Diagnosis
process.description: Identify N+1 problems, missing indexes, over-normalisation or under-normalisation, and caching opportunities.

process.title: Optimisation
process.description: Add indexes, rewrite queries, implement eager loading, introduce Redis caching where appropriate.

process.title: Schema redesign (if needed)
process.description: Design and execute schema migrations with zero-downtime strategies for production systems.

process.title: Benchmarking & documentation
process.description: Before/after query execution time benchmarks, index documentation, and caching layer documentation.

deliverables: Database audit report with prioritised improvement recommendations
Optimised queries and Eloquent scopes
Index strategy implementation and documentation
Redis caching layer configuration
Migration files for any schema changes
Before/after performance benchmarks
outcomes: Measurably faster queries — before/after execution times documented
Application that stays performant as data volume grows — not just today
Reduced database server load — less compute needed for the same traffic
Data integrity enforced at the schema level — fewer bugs from inconsistent data
Development team that understands the schema and can extend it confidently

services.title: Third-Party Integrations
services.description: Connecting your product to the tools your business runs on — payment gateways, CRMs, marketing platforms, analytics, and more. Clean, maintainable integration code that doesn't break when APIs change.
services.detailed_description: Modern products do not operate in isolation — they need to talk to payment processors, CRMs, shipping providers, marketing tools, analytics platforms, and more. I design and build integration layers that are resilient, well-logged, and maintainable. Every integration I write uses the adapter pattern to isolate third-party dependencies — so when Razorpay updates their API or you decide to switch from Mailchimp to another platform, you change one file, not thirty.
services_included: Payment gateway integration (Razorpay, Stripe, PayPal, PayU, Cashfree)
CRM integration (Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho)
Email and marketing platform integration (Mailchimp, SendGrid, NotifyVisitor)
Shipping and logistics API integration (Shiprocket, Delhivery, EasyShip)
SMS and communication integration (Twilio, MSG91, Exotel)
Google services integration (Maps, Analytics, Search Console, OAuth)
Social media API integration (Meta, LinkedIn, Twitter/X)
Accounting software integration (QuickBooks, Tally, Zoho Books)
Webhook development and inbound event processing
benefits: Adapter pattern isolates third-party dependencies — switching providers requires minimal code change
Centrally logged integration events make debugging production issues fast and straightforward
Webhook handling with retry logic and idempotency prevents duplicate processing of events
Clean integration layer reduces onboarding time for new developers who need to understand external dependencies
Proper error handling means your application degrades gracefully when a third-party service is unavailable
use_cases: An e-commerce platform integrating Razorpay for payments, Shiprocket for shipping, and Mailchimp for post-purchase emails
A SaaS CRM syncing leads and contacts with Salesforce bidirectionally via webhooks
A healthcare platform integrating with an SMS gateway for appointment reminders and a payment gateway for consultations
A marketplace needing Google Maps for location features, Google Analytics for behaviour tracking, and OAuth for login
problems_solved: Integration code scattered across the application — impossible to update when the external API changes
No retry logic on webhook endpoints — missed events that are never processed
Payment integration that works in testing but has edge cases that silently fail in production
No logging on integration calls — impossible to diagnose why an order was not fulfilled
Tight coupling to one provider — switching from Stripe to Razorpay requires changes in dozens of files

process.title: Integration mapping
process.description: Map all required integrations, data flows, and event triggers before writing code.

process.title: Adapter design
process.description: Design the integration interface and adapter pattern to isolate each third-party dependency.

process.title: Implementation
process.description: Build each integration with proper authentication, error handling, retry logic, and logging.

process.title: Testing
process.description: Test in sandbox/test mode, validate edge cases (failed payments, webhook retries, rate limits).

process.title: Documentation & monitoring
process.description: Document each integration, log formats, and set up alerting for integration failures.

deliverables: Integration adapter layer for each third-party service
Webhook endpoint implementation with signature validation and retry handling
Integration test suite (sandbox/test mode coverage)
Logging and monitoring for each integration
Integration documentation (credentials, endpoints, event reference)
Error handling and graceful degradation implementation
outcomes: All business-critical systems connected and exchanging data reliably
Integration failures surfaced immediately via alerts — not discovered by users
Clean adapter architecture that makes future provider switches a one-file change
Reduced manual data entry through automated system-to-system sync
Confidence that payment processing, notifications, and data sync all work correctly in production

services.title: Tech Consulting & Rescue
services.description: Auditing stuck or legacy projects, untangling technical debt, and unblocking delivery — honest assessment, clear priorities, and hands-on fixes.
services.detailed_description: I work with teams that are stuck — applications that have grown too complex to change safely, projects that are behind schedule, codebases that the original developer left without documentation, or technical decisions made early that are now causing problems at scale. I audit the situation honestly, prioritise the highest-impact improvements, and either fix them hands-on or give your team a clear, actionable plan to fix them themselves. No fluff, no overpromising — just an accurate diagnosis and a path forward.
services_included: Full technical audit of existing codebase, architecture, and infrastructure
Technical debt assessment and prioritised remediation roadmap
Performance bottleneck diagnosis (application, database, server)
Security vulnerability audit and remediation plan
Code review and architecture review for in-progress projects
Legacy PHP / WordPress rescue and modernisation
Team unblocking — fixing the specific problem stopping delivery
Technology stack evaluation and migration planning
Developer onboarding documentation for codebases without it
industries: Companies Preparing for Investment or Acquisition
Any Team Whose Application Has Become Hard to Change
Projects That Have Gone Over Time and Budget
benefits: An outside perspective surfaces problems the team is too close to see — or too afraid to say out loud
Prioritised remediation roadmap means you fix the right things first, not just the most visible ones
Hands-on fixes deliver immediate relief — not just a report that sits in a drawer
Honest assessment of what is actually wrong — no vendor interest in recommending a full rewrite if a targeted fix will do
Documentation deliverable means your team can maintain and extend the codebase after the engagement ends
use_cases: A startup whose Laravel application has become slow and unmaintainable as the team has grown and features have been bolted on
An agency that has inherited a client codebase with no documentation and a deadline approaching
A business whose original developer has left and the replacement team cannot understand the application
A company whose WordPress site has accumulated years of plugin bloat, security vulnerabilities, and performance debt
problems_solved: No one on the team understands how the whole system works — tribal knowledge walked out the door
Every new feature takes three times longer than it should because the existing code resists change
Application is slow but no one knows exactly why — no monitoring, no profiling, no baseline
Security audit revealed vulnerabilities but the team does not know how to prioritise or fix them
Project is behind schedule and the team needs someone to diagnose why and help them recover

process.title: Rapid assessment
process.description: Review the codebase, architecture, deployment environment, and any existing documentation. Identify the highest-risk areas.

process.title: Findings report
process.description: Deliver a written report with a clear diagnosis, prioritised issues, and specific recommendations — not generalities.

process.title: Remediation planning
process.description: Define a phased plan to address the highest-impact issues first without requiring a full application rewrite.

process.title: Hands-on fixes
process.description: Implement the agreed fixes directly — performance improvements, security hardening, documentation, refactoring.

process.title: Handover
process.description: Transfer knowledge to the internal team with documentation, pair programming sessions, and a clear ongoing maintenance guide.

deliverables: Technical audit report with prioritised findings and recommendations
Remediation roadmap with time and effort estimates
Hands-on fixes for agreed highest-priority issues
Codebase documentation (architecture overview, setup guide, key decisions)
Performance benchmarks before and after remediation
Security hardening implementation and report
outcomes: Team unblocked — able to develop and deploy with confidence again
Clear understanding of what the application does and how — no more tribal knowledge risk
Measurable performance improvement on the worst-performing pages or queries
Security vulnerabilities addressed and documented
A prioritised roadmap the team can execute independently without consultant dependency
